text:
Request rejection
Requests can be rejected by the rate limiter if the underlying token bucket is out of tokens. When
an operation is rejected, it will use a new IngressAdmissionRejected error code along with the
SystemOverloaded error label. No other retryable error labels will be appended as part of this
project, though that may be revisited at a later date as part of future Load Shedding projects.
Configuration
The following new server parameters will be introduced:
• ingressRequestRateLimiterEnabled: bool, (default false), determines whether the rate limiter is
enabled or not
• ingressRequestAdmissionRatePerSec: integer (default unlimited), expresses the number of
requests that can be admitted per second (default: unlimited)
• ingressRequestAdmissionBurstCapacitySecs: integer (default: n/a, must be specified if rate
limit specified), Describes how many seconds worth of unutilized rate limit can be stored away for
use during periods where the rate limit is temporarily exceeded.
• ingressRequestRateLimiterBypass: array of strings (default empty), a list of CIDR ranges to be
exempted from ingress admission control rate limiting
